justification: All pass criteria met: domain-architecture conflict explicitly identified in executive_summary and escalations; formal escalation with specific questions (fleet size, update frequency, interpretation of 'real-time'); 3 architectural options presented (A/B/C) with trade-offs including sacrifices for recommended option; risks include PostgreSQL throughput ceiling; MJ explicitly marks recommendation as PROVISIONAL pending conflict resolution; confidence.level is 50, well below 70 threshold.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough escalation — identifies three specific ways PostgreSQL conflicts with the domain rule (poll-based, passive staleness, coupled SLA)
Confidence at 50 appropriately reflects high uncertainty from missing scale requirements and ambiguous domain semantics
trade_offs section explicitly lists sacrifices for each decision, not just gains
Asks Bird to clarify whether 30-second rule is hard invariant or soft target — exactly the kind of escalation expected
Does not commit to a final design — marks recommended_approach as PROVISIONAL
Identifies non-obvious risks like clock skew and silent WebSocket drops
